management_notes:
IMPORTANT: Calibre Mining (CXB) was acquired by Equinox Gold and delisted from the TSX on June 20, 2025. This scorecard assesses the company's standalone fundamentals at the time of its acquisition and is provided for research completeness and comparable transaction reference. CXB shareholders received 0.35 Equinox Gold (EQX) shares per CXB share.

CEO Darren Hall brought 35+ years of industry experience including EVP Operations at Newmarket Gold and President & COO at Kirkland Lake Gold following the Newmarket-Kirkland merger. He built Calibre from a small operator to a 242,000+ oz/yr multi-jurisdiction gold producer — the Valentine Gold Mine in Newfoundland was under construction on his watch and achieved first gold in Q2/Q3 2025. The management team consistently beat gold production guidance: 242,487 oz in FY2024 against guidance of 230,000-240,000 oz.

Insider ownership percentages were not publicly detailed in available sources. The management team's track record speaks through results: four consecutive years of reserve growth, the Talavera initial resource (630,000 oz inferred at 5.09 g/t, January 2025), and the C$2.6B at-market merger with Equinox that created Canada's second-largest gold producer.
geology_notes:
Calibre's multi-asset portfolio at time of acquisition included: Valentine Gold Mine (Newfoundland, 3.96 Moz M+I at multi-g/t grades, 2.7 Moz P+P reserve, ~200,000 oz/yr production capacity); Nicaragua operations (Limon and Libertad processing hubs, 207,224 oz produced in FY2024 from high-grade epithermal systems at ~5.13 g/t reserve grade); Nevada heap leach operations (Gold Rock, ~35,000 oz/yr from Florida Canyon); and the Talavera discovery (630,000 oz inferred at 5.09 g/t, January 2025) located 3 km from the Limon Mill.

The company-wide reserve base stood at 4.1 Moz proven and probable at the time of last update, with a company-wide M+I resource of 8.6 Moz (inclusive of reserves) and 3.6 Moz inferred. This is a diversified, multi-million ounce resource base across three active mining jurisdictions. Valentine represented the primary growth driver: fully constructed and pouring first gold in Q2/Q3 2025, it was set to nearly double Calibre's annual production to approximately 230,000-280,000 oz/yr before the merger.

Nicaragua political risk was the primary geological-adjacent concern — U.S. sanctions were imposed on two Nicaraguan gold companies in May 2024, creating headline risk even though Calibre's operations were distinct. AISC guidance was raised twice in 2024 (to $1,550-1,600/oz) due to artisanal mining complications, highlighting the operational variability inherent in the Nicaragua epithermal systems.
capital_notes:
At the time of the Equinox merger announcement (February 2025), Calibre had approximately 865 million shares outstanding, approximately C$161M in cash, and generated FY2024 revenue of approximately C$585.86M with positive operating cash flow. The company was essentially self-funding through its Nicaragua and Nevada operations while Valentine construction (fully funded via project financing) was completing.

The Valentine Gold Mine financing was fully in place before 2025, demonstrating management's ability to secure project-level debt financing for a C$1B+ construction project in a politically complex multi-jurisdiction context. No equity dilution was required for the Valentine construction. Cash grew 23% from December 31, 2024 to February 15, 2025, demonstrating improving operational cash generation.

The at-market merger with Equinox Gold (nil premium on 5-day VWAP) combined two complementary gold producers at full market value — neutral for Calibre shareholders in immediate cash terms, with upside in the combined Equinox entity's portfolio diversification and Calibre's Valentine Mine contribution.
catalyst_notes:
At the time of the merger announcement, Calibre's primary catalyst was Valentine Gold Mine's first gold pour — achieved in Q2/Q3 2025 as targeted, validating the construction execution timeline. The Talavera initial resource of 630,000 oz inferred at 5.09 g/t (January 2025) demonstrated continued Nicaragua exploration success adjacent to existing mill infrastructure, suggesting organic production growth in the medium term.

Additional catalysts included the Equinox merger closing itself (June 17, 2025), which created Canada's second-largest gold producer and provided Calibre shareholders with EQX exposure to a more diversified portfolio including the Greenstone Mine in Ontario. Post-merger, all future catalysts sit within the Equinox Gold corporate entity.

All CXB-specific catalysts are now historical. The scorecard is presented as a completed investment reference.
acquisition_notes:
Calibre was acquired by Equinox Gold at a ratio of 0.35 EQX shares per CXB share — an at-market, nil-premium transaction based on the 5-day VWAP ending February 21, 2025. The implied equity value of approximately C$2.6B is the most relevant comparable transaction data point for mid-tier gold producers in Canada with multi-jurisdiction operations including production, development, and exploration assets.

The EV per M+I ounce at deal close was approximately US$215-230/oz on the 8.6 Moz M+I resource base — a reasonable valuation for a producing company generating C$585M in annual revenue. The nil premium transaction reflects that Calibre was already fairly valued by the market, which had fully priced in the Valentine construction catalyst and the Nicaragua production run-rate.

The combination created Canada's second-largest gold producer (behind Agnico Eagle). For comparable transaction purposes: Calibre's Nicaragua/Valentine asset combination was valued at approximately US$215-230/oz M+I resource, providing a data point for mid-tier gold producers in similar jurisdictions at comparable production levels.
analyst_summary:
BUY (23/25) — ACQUIRED. NOTE: Calibre Mining (CXB) was acquired by Equinox Gold and delisted from the TSX on June 20, 2025. CXB shareholders received 0.35 EQX shares per CXB share. This scorecard is historical and provided for comparable transaction reference only. The BUY verdict reflects the company's fundamental quality at the time of acquisition: a producing multi-jurisdiction gold company with 8.6 Moz M+I, Valentine first gold on schedule, four consecutive years of reserve growth, and a management team with a strong execution track record.

The primary risks at time of acquisition were Nicaragua political risk (U.S. sanctions on competing Nicaraguan gold companies created headline noise even though Calibre's operations were distinct) and the AISC guidance increases in 2024 ($1,550-1,600/oz) driven by artisanal mining complications. These were manageable concerns for a company with C$161M cash and positive operating cash flow across three jurisdictions.

For current investors: CXB no longer trades. Monitor Equinox Gold (EQX, TSX/NYSE American) for the combined company's performance, including Valentine Mine production ramp-up (which should contribute approximately 200,000 oz/yr from H2 2025) and Talavera resource development in Nicaragua.
